items.title: POLARIS: Guiding Small Models to Write Long Stories
items.description:
arXiv:2606.04095v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Small open-weight models struggle at long-form creative writing: their generated stories either fall far short of the requested length, or their quality significantly degrades as length increases, especially when compared to frontier models. We presen...

items.title: Discourse-Role Labels as Presentation-Time Variables for Context Use in Language Models
items.description:
arXiv:2606.04109v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Context-augmented language model systems often wrap supplied content with labels such as Reference:, Evidence:, Instruction:, Note:, or Example:, but the effect of these labels on reader-model behavior remains underexplored. We introduce a paired fixe...

items.title: Computational conceptual history of scientific concepts: From early digital methods to LLMs
items.description:
arXiv:2606.04118v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This article situates large language models (LLMs) within the longer history of computational approaches to concept analysis in the history, philosophy, and sociology of science (HPSS). We examine what LLMs add to existing methods, how they inherit lo...

arXiv:2606.04120v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Conversational agents that serve as lifelong companions must maintain persistent memory across all interactions. However, simply expanding context windows with raw retrieval degrades reasoning quality, while training memory agents via standard reinfor...

items.title: When Retrieval Doesn't Help: A Large-Scale Study of Biomedical RAG
items.description:
arXiv:2606.04127v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Medical question answering is a high-stakes setting where factual errors can have serious consequences. Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is widely viewed as a promising solution, and prior work has reported substantial gains for large medical QA m...

items.title: Expert-Aware Refusal Steering
items.description:
arXiv:2606.04160v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Safety alignment in instruction-tuned large language models (LLMs) depends on a model's ability to reliably refuse to respond to harmful or disallowed requests. Recent work has shown that a steering vector can be applied to a dense LLM during inferenc...

items.description:
arXiv:2606.04177v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Interpretable linguistic features offer a promising approach for explaining why a given text appears machine-generated, particularly for non-expert users. However, existing findings on which features reliably indicate LLM-generated text remain fragmen...

items.description:
arXiv:2606.04199v1 Announce Type: new

items.title: MM-BizRAG: Rethinking Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation for General Purpose Enterprise Q&A
items.description:

Abstract: Recent advances in multimodal retrieval-augmented generation (MM-RAG) have shifted toward minimal parsing, relying on page-level images for producing retriever embeddings and for answer generation. While efficient, this trend often neglects explicit h...

items.description:

Abstract: Pre-deployment verification of enterprise artificial intelligence (AI) agents remains a critical gap between large language model (LLM) capability benchmarking and production deployment. Post-deployment monitoring, human-in-the-loop controls, and prom...

items.description:

Abstract: Large language models are reshaping research practice while quietly eroding researchers epistemic accountability. This commentary introduces PEEL - Protocols for Epistemically Engaged Literacy in AI, a working scaffolding that combines deterministic d...

Abstract: Existing quantization methods are fundamentally limited by rigid, integer-based bit-widths (e.g., 2, 3-bit), resulting in a ``deployment gap" where Large Language Models cannot be optimally fitted to specific memory budgets. To bridge this gap, we int...

items.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.

items.title: code-review-graph - Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.

items.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
items.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw).
